question: 1.Create a table called STUDENT with columns: SID (NUMBER) , SNAME (VARCHAR2(30)) , AGE (NUMBER) , COURSE (VARCHAR2(20))
query: CREATE TABLE STUDENT (SID NUMBER, SNAME VARCHAR2(30), AGE NUMBER, COURSE VARCHAR2(20));

question: 2. Create a table EMPLOYEE with columns: EID NUMBER , ENAME VARCHAR2(40) , SALARY NUMBER(8,2) , DEPTNO NUMBER
query: CREATE TABLE EMPLOYEE (EID NUMBER, ENAME VARCHAR2(40), SALARY NUMBER(8,2), DEPTNO NUMBER);

question: 3. Create a table DEPARTMENT with columns: DEPTNO NUMBER , DNAME VARCHAR2(30) , LOCATION VARCHAR2(30)
query: CREATE TABLE DEPARTMENT (DEPTNO NUMBER, DNAME VARCHAR2(30), LOCATION VARCHAR2(30));

question: 4. Create a table BOOK with columns: BOOK_ID NUMBER , TITLE VARCHAR2(50) , AUTHOR VARCHAR2(40) , PRICE NUMBER
query: CREATE TABLE BOOK (BOOK_ID NUMBER, TITLE VARCHAR2(50), AUTHOR VARCHAR2(40), PRICE NUMBER);

question: 5. Create a table COURSE with columns: CID NUMBER , CNAME VARCHAR2(40) , DURATION NUMBER
query: CREATE TABLE COURSE (CID NUMBER, CNAME VARCHAR2(40), DURATION NUMBER);

question: 6. Create a table PROJECT with columns: PID NUMBER , PNAME VARCHAR2(50) , START_DATE DATE , END_DATE DATE
query: CREATE TABLE PROJECT (PID NUMBER, PNAME VARCHAR2(50), START_DATE DATE, END_DATE DATE);

question: 7. Create a table CUSTOMER with columns: CID NUMBER , CNAME VARCHAR2(40) , CITY VARCHAR2(30) , PHONE NUMBER
query: CREATE TABLE CUSTOMER (CID NUMBER, CNAME VARCHAR2(40), CITY VARCHAR2(30), PHONE NUMBER);

question: 8. Create a table SALES with columns: SALE_ID NUMBER , PRODUCT_NAME VARCHAR2(50) , QUANTITY NUMBER , PRICE NUMBER
query: CREATE TABLE SALES (SALE_ID NUMBER, PRODUCT_NAME VARCHAR2(50), QUANTITY NUMBER, PRICE NUMBER);

question: 9. Create a table EMP_COPY from the existing EMP table (structure only)
query: CREATE TABLE EMP_COPY AS SELECT * FROM EMP WHERE 1=0;

question: 10. Create a table EMP_BACKUP from EMP including data
query: CREATE TABLE EMP_BACKUP AS SELECT * FROM EMP;

question: 11. Add a column EMAIL VARCHAR2(50) to the STUDENT table
12. Add a column PHONE NUMBER to the EMPLOYEE table
13. Modify the column SNAME in STUDENT table to VARCHAR2(50)
14. Modify the column SALARY in EMPLOYEE table to NUMBER(10,2)
15. Add two columns CITY VARCHAR2(30) and PINCODE NUMBER to CUSTOMER table
query: ALTER TABLE CUSTOMER ADD (CITY VARCHAR2(30), PINCODE NUMBER);

question: 16. Rename the column SNAME to STUDENT_NAME in STUDENT table
query: ALTER TABLE STUDENT RENAME COLUMN SNAME TO STUDENT_NAME;

question: 17. Drop the column AGE from STUDENT table
query: ALTER TABLE STUDENT DROP COLUMN AGE;

question: 18. Set the column SALARY in EMPLOYEE table to NOT NULL
query: ALTER TABLE EMPLOYEE MODIFY (SALARY NOT NULL);

question: 19. Increase the size of column TITLE in BOOK table to VARCHAR2(100)
20. Add a column MANAGER_ID NUMBER to EMPLOYEE table
21. Drop the table STUDENT
22. Drop the table CUSTOMER from the database
23. Drop the table PROJECT permanently
24. Remove the table SALES
25. Drop the table COURSE
26. Rename the table STUDENT to STUDENTS
27. Rename the table EMPLOYEE to EMP_MASTER
28. Rename the table CUSTOMER to CLIENT
29. Rename the table BOOK to BOOK_DETAILS
30. Rename the table PROJECT to PROJECT_INFO
31. Remove all records from the STUDENT table
32. Delete all rows from EMPLOYEE table using TRUNCATE
33. Empty the CUSTOMER table quickly
34. Remove all data from SALES table but keep structure
35. Clear the PROJECT table
36. Create a table TRAINING with columns TID , TNAME , DURATION
query: CREATE TABLE TRAINING (TID NUMBER, TNAME VARCHAR2(30), DURATION NUMBER);

question: 37. Add column TRAINER_NAME VARCHAR2(40) to TRAINING
38. Modify TNAME column size to VARCHAR2(60)
39. Rename the TRAINING table to TRAINING_PROGRAM
40. Truncate the TRAINING_PROGRAM table
41. Drop the TRAINING_PROGRAM table
42. Create a table EMP_PROJECT with the following columns: EMP_ID NUMBER , PROJECT_ID NUMBER , START_DATE DATE , END_DATE DATE , ROLE VARCHAR2(30).
query: CREATE TABLE EMP_PROJECT (EMP_ID NUMBER, PROJECT_ID NUMBER, START_DATE DATE, END_DATE DATE, ROLE VARCHAR2(30));

question: 42.1 Later modify the table to increase the size of ROLE to VARCHAR2(50).
42.2 Add a column STATUS VARCHAR2(20).
43. Create a table PRODUCT with columns PID NUMBER , PNAME VARCHAR2(50) , PRICE NUMBER , CATEGORY VARCHAR2(30).
query: CREATE TABLE PRODUCT (PID NUMBER, PNAME VARCHAR2(50), PRICE NUMBER, CATEGORY VARCHAR2(30));

question: 43.1 Then rename column PNAME to PRODUCT_NAME.
query: ALTER TABLE PRODUCT RENAME COLUMN PNAME TO PRODUCT_NAME;

question: 43.2 Change PRICE datatype to NUMBER(10,2).
44. Create a table ORDERS with columns ORDER_ID NUMBER , CUSTOMER_ID NUMBER , ORDER_DATE DATE , TOTAL_AMOUNT NUMBER.
query: CREATE TABLE ORDERS (ORDER_ID NUMBER, CUSTOMER_ID NUMBER, ORDER_DATE DATE, TOTAL_AMOUNT NUMBER);

question: 44.1 After creation: Add column STATUS VARCHAR2(20).
44.2 Drop column TOTAL_AMOUNT.
query: ALTER TABLE ORDERS DROP COLUMN TOTAL_AMOUNT;

question: 44.3 Add column TOTAL_AMOUNT NUMBER(10,2) again.
45. Create a table EMP_TEMP from the EMP table containing only employees with salary greater than 2000.
query: CREATE TABLE EMP_TEMP AS SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEE WHERE SALARY > 2000;

question: 45.1 Then: Rename the table to EMP_HIGH_SALARY.
45.2 Truncate the table.
45.3 Drop the table.
46. Create a table STUDENT_RECORD with columns SID NUMBER , SNAME VARCHAR2(40) , COURSE VARCHAR2(30) , MARKS NUMBER.
query: CREATE TABLE STUDENT_RECORD (SID NUMBER, SNAME VARCHAR2(40), COURSE VARCHAR2(30), MARKS NUMBER);

question: 46.1 Add column GRADE CHAR(1).
46.2 Modify MARKS datatype to NUMBER(5,2).
46.3 Rename column COURSE to COURSE_NAME.
query: ALTER TABLE STUDENT_RECORD RENAME COLUMN COURSE TO COURSE_NAME;

question: 47. Create a table EMP_ARCHIVE using the structure of the EMP table but without copying data.
query: CREATE TABLE EMP_ARCHIVE AS SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEE WHERE 1=0;

question: 47.1 Then: Add column ARCHIVE_DATE DATE.
47.2 Rename the table to EMP_HISTORY.
48. Create a table DEPT_BACKUP from DEPT table including all rows.
query: CREATE TABLE DEPT_BACKUP AS SELECT * FROM DEPARTMENT;

question: 48.1 Then: Drop column LOC.
query: ALTER TABLE DEPT_BACKUP DROP COLUMN LOCATION;

question: 48.2 Add column LOCATION VARCHAR2(40).
49. Create a table SALES_DATA with columns SALE_ID NUMBER , PRODUCT_ID NUMBER , SALE_DATE DATE , AMOUNT NUMBER.
query: CREATE TABLE SALES_DATA (SALE_ID NUMBER, PRODUCT_ID NUMBER, SALE_DATE DATE, AMOUNT NUMBER);

question: 49.1 Add column REGION VARCHAR2(30).
49.2 Modify AMOUNT to NUMBER(12,2).
49.3 Rename the table to REGIONAL_SALES_DATA.
50. Create a table LOG_TABLE with columns LOG_ID NUMBER , USER_NAME VARCHAR2(30) , ACTION VARCHAR2(50) , LOG_DATE DATE.
query: CREATE TABLE LOG_TABLE (LOG_ID NUMBER, USER_NAME VARCHAR2(30), ACTION VARCHAR2(50), LOG_DATE DATE);

question: 50.1 Then: Truncate the table.
50.2 Rename it to SYSTEM_LOG.
50.3 And drop it permanently.
